Container.Name: host-agent
Container.Overview:
Single-container host agent for the pq fleet.
Bundles node-exporter, cadvisor, smartctl-exporter, ipmi-exporter, nvidia-gpu-exporter, the Dell PowerEdge fan controller, and vmagent.
vmagent scrapes every sub-exporter on localhost and pushes the samples to the central Prometheus at docker-1.internal.pq.io:9090. external_labels.host is set to this box's kernel hostname, so the dashboard sees a new host the moment its first push lands — no central config changes needed.
Sub-exporters that need hardware not present on this host (ipmi-exporter without /dev/ipmi0, nvidia-gpu-exporter without nvidia-smi, etc.) self-disable. Same image runs on Dell, Mac mini, plain Linux + Docker, or Unraid.

Config.Name: Host filesystem
Config.Description: Read-only view of the host filesystem. node-exporter reads /host/proc, /host/sys, /host/var/run/docker.sock, /host/var/lib/docker through this single mount — leave the default.

Config.Name: Host devices
Config.Description: Direct passthrough of host /dev. ipmitool and smartctl talk to /dev/ipmi0 and /dev/sd* / /dev/bus/0 directly — Docker doesn't auto-share host devices even with Privileged on. Leave the default.

Config.Name: State directory
Config.Description: Persistent state for the Dell fan controller's EWMA baseline (takes 24–48 h to learn) and the vmagent write-ahead log. Safe to keep as the default; safe to leave empty if you're OK with state being reset every container restart.

Label.Name: watchtower
Label.Description: Marks the container as eligible for the watchtower auto-updater on this LAN.

Container.Label: com.centurylinklabs.watchtower.enable=true
